title: erianthus ravennae subsp. parviflorus
intro: Erianthus ravennae subsp. parviflorus, also known as Little Bluestem, is a perennial grass species native to North America, characterized by its delicate blue-green foliage and small purple flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Erianthus ravennae subsp. parviflorus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, lower blood pressure, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
